purpose: Spot-check whether the skill consistently drives safe, incremental refactoring behavior.
rubric: Opens with goal, preserved behavior, plan, and verification.
Keeps refactoring separate from behavior changes unless the user explicitly combines them.
Uses small, reviewable slices instead of broad rewrites.
Mentions a realistic safety net when tests are weak or behavior is unclear.
Avoids unnecessary public API changes and unrelated cleanup.

cases.name: duplicate-logic-extraction
cases.prompt: Refactor these two handlers. They both parse the same payload and run the same validation before calling different services.
should_mention: existing handler tests or narrow request checks
should_avoid: change endpoint behavior without calling it out

cases.name: legacy-module-with-weak-tests
cases.prompt: Please refactor this old reporting module. It is brittle, and our test coverage here is poor.
should_mention: characterization check or narrow safety net
reduced scope if behavior is unclear
should_avoid: claiming strong safety without a check

cases.name: mixed-refactor-and-feature-change
cases.prompt: Refactor this retry service and also change it so network timeouts get one extra retry.
should_mention: separating refactor work from the behavior change
keeping current retry behavior stable during the refactor phase
separate verification for the new retry rule
should_avoid: blending the retry rule change into the same unstructured step
